technical.question: What is the difference between a stack and a queue? Give a real-world use case for each.
key_points: Stack use case: function call stack or undo/redo
Queue use case: print queue or task scheduling

technical.question: Explain the difference between process and thread. When would you use multithreading?
key_points: Process has its own memory space
Threads share memory within a process

technical.question: What is Big O notation? What is the time complexity of binary search?
key_points: Big O describes worst-case time/space growth
Divides search space in half each step

technical.question: What is the difference between SQL and NoSQL databases? When would you choose one over the other?
key_points: SQL is structured with fixed schema
NoSQL for unstructured or high-scale data

technical.question: Explain what REST API is and what makes an API RESTful.
key_points: HTTP methods: GET POST PUT DELETE

technical.question: What is the difference between HTTP and HTTPS? How does SSL/TLS work at a high level?
Design a URL shortener like bit.ly. Walk through your system design choices.
key_points: Hash function to generate short code

technical.question: What is a deadlock? How can you prevent it?
key_points: Deadlock: two processes waiting for each other's resources
Four conditions: mutual exclusion hold-and-wait no preemption circular wait

technical.question: Explain the concept of CAP theorem in distributed systems.
key_points: Can only guarantee two of three

technical.question: How does garbage collection work in Java or Python? What are its trade-offs?
key_points: Mark and sweep or reference counting
Generational GC optimises for short-lived objects

behavioral.question: Tell me about a time you had to debug a critical bug in production. How did you approach it?
Describe a situation where you disagreed with a technical decision made by your team. What did you do?
key_points: Backed opinion with data or reasoning

behavioral.question: Tell me about a time you had to learn a new technology quickly under a deadline.
key_points: Broke down learning into small chunks

behavioral.question: Give an example of a time you improved the performance of an existing system.

situational.question: Your code review reveals a major flaw in a feature that ships in 2 hours. What do you do?
You are assigned a task with unclear requirements. How do you proceed?
key_points: Get early feedback before going deep

situational.question: A junior developer on your team keeps submitting low-quality code. How would you handle this?

technical.question: What is the difference between == and === in JavaScript?
key_points: Example: 0 == false is true but 0 === false is false

technical.question: Explain the concept of the virtual DOM in React. Why does it exist?
What is CSS specificity and how is it calculated?
key_points: Later rules win if specificity is equal

technical.question: Explain event delegation in JavaScript with an example.
key_points: Attach one listener to parent instead of each child
Check event.target to identify the element

technical.question: What are React hooks? Explain useState and useEffect.
key_points: Hooks let functional components use state and lifecycle
useState returns state value and setter
useEffect runs after render for side effects
Dependency array controls when effect re-runs

technical.question: What is CORS and how do you handle it in a frontend application?
key_points: Preflight OPTIONS request for complex requests

technical.question: How would you optimise the performance of a slow React application?
key_points: useMemo and useCallback for expensive computations
Code splitting with lazy and Suspense

behavioral.question: Describe a UI feature you built that you are most proud of. What made it challenging?
Tell me about a time you had to make a complex UI accessible. What steps did you take?

situational.question: A designer hands you a mockup that is technically very difficult to implement exactly. How do you handle it?
Your page loads slowly on mobile. Walk me through how you would diagnose and fix it.
key_points: Check network waterfall for large assets
Optimise images and use lazy loading

technical.question: What is the difference between authentication and authorisation?
key_points: Authorisation: verifying permissions (what you can do)
JWT is commonly used for authentication

technical.question: Explain database indexing. When would you add an index and when would you not?
key_points: Slows down writes due to index maintenance
Avoid on columns with high write frequency or low cardinality

technical.question: What is connection pooling and why is it important in a backend service?
key_points: Reuses database connections instead of creating new ones

technical.question: Explain the difference between synchronous and asynchronous programming. Give a backend example.
key_points: Async allows other work while waiting
Useful for I/O bound tasks like DB calls or API requests

technical.question: How would you design a rate limiter for an API?
key_points: Token bucket or sliding window algorithm
Store state in Redis for distributed systems
Apply per user or per IP

behavioral.question: Tell me about a time you optimised a slow database query. What was your process?
key_points: Identified slow query using EXPLAIN or logs

behavioral.question: Describe a situation where you had to design an API consumed by multiple clients.

situational.question: Your API is returning 500 errors in production for 10% of requests. How do you investigate?
A third-party API your service depends on goes down. How do you handle it?
key_points: Alert team and monitor status page

technical.question: What is the difference between Docker and a virtual machine?
key_points: Containers are lighter and faster to start

technical.question: Explain the CI/CD pipeline. What happens at each stage?
key_points: CI: code pushed triggers build and tests
CD: automated deployment to staging or production

technical.question: What is Kubernetes and what problem does it solve?
What is Infrastructure as Code (IaC)? Name a tool and explain how you have used it.
key_points: Manage infrastructure via code not manual steps

technical.question: How would you monitor a production application? What metrics would you track?
key_points: CPU memory disk and network usage

behavioral.question: Tell me about a production incident you handled. What was your role and what did you learn?

situational.question: A deployment just went out and error rates spiked to 40%. What do you do in the first 5 minutes?
key_points: Do not push more changes until stable

situational.question: Your team wants to migrate from monolith to microservices. What risks and steps would you consider?
key_points: Strangler fig pattern for gradual migration

technical.question: What is the difference between symmetric and asymmetric encryption? Give an example of each.
key_points: Symmetric uses same key for encrypt and decrypt: AES
Asymmetric uses public/private key pair: RSA
Asymmetric used for key exchange like in TLS

technical.question: Explain what a SQL injection attack is and how you prevent it.
key_points: Attacker injects SQL through user input
Can read modify or delete database data
Input validation and least privilege DB access

technical.question: What is the OWASP Top 10? Name at least 3 vulnerabilities from it.
What is a man-in-the-middle attack and how do you defend against it?
key_points: Attacker intercepts communication between two parties
Can read or modify data in transit

technical.question: What is the difference between IDS and IPS?
key_points: IDS: Intrusion Detection System — monitors and alerts
IPS: Intrusion Prevention System — blocks threats in real time
IDS is passive IPS is active
IPS sits inline in network traffic path

technical.question: Explain how a buffer overflow attack works.
key_points: Writing data beyond allocated buffer boundary
Overwrites adjacent memory including return address
Attacker redirects execution to malicious code
Prevention: bounds checking ASLR stack canaries

behavioral.question: Describe a time you identified a security vulnerability in a system. What did you do?

situational.question: You discover an employee has been accessing files outside their role. How do you handle it?
Your company's website is experiencing a DDoS attack. Walk through your response.
key_points: Scale up or enable rate limiting

technical.question: What is the difference between supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning?
What is overfitting? How do you detect and prevent it?
key_points: Model memorises training data, fails on new data
Detect: high train accuracy low validation accuracy

technical.question: Explain the bias-variance trade-off.
key_points: High bias: underfitting, model too simple
High variance: overfitting, model too complex
Goal: minimise both for good generalisation
Regularisation reduces variance, increasing bias slightly

technical.question: What is the purpose of a train-validation-test split? Why not just use train and test?
key_points: Using test for tuning causes data leakage

technical.question: How does backpropagation work in a neural network?
key_points: Calculate gradient of loss with respect to weights using chain rule
Update weights in direction that reduces loss

technical.question: What is attention mechanism in transformers and why was it a breakthrough?
key_points: Allows model to weigh importance of each token relative to others
Replaces sequential RNN with parallel computation
Foundation of BERT GPT and modern LLMs

behavioral.question: Tell me about an ML project you built end-to-end. What was the hardest part?

situational.question: Your model performs well in testing but poorly in production. What could be the reasons?

technical.question: What is the difference between INNER JOIN, LEFT JOIN, and FULL OUTER JOIN?
key_points: INNER JOIN returns only matching rows
LEFT JOIN returns all left rows plus matches from right
FULL OUTER JOIN returns all rows from both tables

technical.question: How would you find duplicate records in a SQL table?
key_points: Remove duplicates with DELETE using CTE

technical.question: What is a window function in SQL? Give an example.
key_points: Performs calculation across related rows without collapsing them
ROW_NUMBER RANK LAG LEAD SUM OVER
Does not reduce rows like GROUP BY

technical.question: Explain the difference between OLTP and OLAP systems.
What is a cohort analysis and when would you use it?
key_points: Groups users by a shared characteristic at a point in time
Common for retention and churn analysis
Example: users who signed up in January — how many active after 30 60 90 days

technical.question: How do you handle missing values in a dataset?
key_points: Understand why data is missing (MCAR MAR MNAR)
Impute with mean median mode or model
Flag as a separate category for categorical data

behavioral.question: Tell me about an insight you found in data that drove a business decision.
Describe a time a stakeholder disagreed with your analysis. How did you handle it?
key_points: Communicated clearly and found common ground

situational.question: You receive a dataset with 30% missing values in a key column. How do you proceed?
key_points: Check if missingness is random or systematic
Decide between imputation or exclusion based on context
Document the decision and its impact on analysis

situational.question: A business team asks for a dashboard but has no clear metric in mind. How do you approach this?
key_points: Ask what decision the dashboard should support
Identify KPIs tied to business goals
Build a prototype and get feedback early
Avoid adding too many metrics at once

technical.question: What is the difference between precision and recall? When would you prioritise one over the other?
key_points: Precision: of all predicted positive how many are actually positive
Recall: of all actual positive how many did we catch
Prioritise recall when false negatives are costly (disease detection)
Prioritise precision when false positives are costly (spam filter)

technical.question: Explain p-value in simple terms. What does p < 0.05 mean?
key_points: Probability of observing result if null hypothesis is true
p < 0.05 means less than 5% chance result is due to chance
Significance level is chosen before the test

technical.question: What is the difference between bagging and boosting?
key_points: Bagging: train models in parallel on random subsets (Random Forest)
Boosting: train models sequentially each correcting previous errors (XGBoost)

technical.question: How would you explain a machine learning model's prediction to a non-technical stakeholder?
key_points: Use SHAP or LIME for feature importance
Translate feature weights into business language
Focus on top 3 factors that influenced the prediction

behavioral.question: Describe a project where your model did not perform as expected. What did you learn?
key_points: Honest account of what went wrong
Key takeaway and how you applied it later

situational.question: You have limited labelled data for a classification task. What approaches would you consider?
key_points: Active learning to label most informative samples

technical.question: What is the difference between a data warehouse and a data lake?
key_points: Data warehouse: structured processed data for analytics
Data lake: raw data in any format

technical.question: Explain what ETL means and describe each step.
key_points: Extract: pull data from source systems
Transform: clean enrich and reshape data
Load: write to destination data store
ELT is a modern variant where transform happens after loading

technical.question: What is Apache Spark and what problem does it solve compared to Hadoop MapReduce?
key_points: Unified API for SQL ML streaming graph

technical.question: How do you handle late-arriving data in a streaming pipeline?
key_points: Watermarking to define how late data can arrive
Event time vs processing time distinction

behavioral.question: Tell me about a data pipeline you built. What was the biggest engineering challenge?
key_points: Challenge: reliability latency or data quality
How you solved it and monitored it

situational.question: Your pipeline has been silently producing incorrect data for 3 days. How do you handle it?
key_points: Backfill correct data and add data quality checks

technical.question: What is the difference between recruitment and talent acquisition?
key_points: TA includes employer branding and pipeline building
TA is proactive recruitment is reactive

technical.question: What is an Employee Value Proposition (EVP) and why does it matter?
key_points: What the company offers employees in exchange for their skills
Includes culture growth compensation and benefits
Directly impacts hiring and retention quality

technical.question: Explain the difference between performance management and performance appraisal.
key_points: Appraisal is one component of management

technical.question: What are the key components of a good onboarding programme?
key_points: Role clarity and goal-setting in first 30 days

behavioral.question: Tell me about a time you had to handle a sensitive employee complaint. How did you manage it?
Describe a situation where you had to convince a hiring manager to change their selection criteria.

situational.question: A high-performing employee comes to you saying they are unhappy and considering leaving. What do you do?
key_points: Identify root cause: compensation, growth, manager, culture
Discuss options: role change, raise, project shift

situational.question: You need to lay off 10% of the workforce. How do you approach the process humanely?
key_points: Communicate clearly and privately to each person
Offer severance outplacement support and references

technical.question: What is the difference between a product roadmap and a product backlog?
key_points: Roadmap: high-level strategic plan over time
Backlog: prioritised list of features and tasks
Backlog is for the development team's day-to-day work

technical.question: How do you decide which feature to build next?
key_points: Use a prioritisation framework: RICE ICE MoSCoW
Consider business value customer impact and effort
Validate with user research and data

technical.question: What is a North Star Metric and how do you choose one?
key_points: Single metric that best captures core product value delivered to users
Aligns entire team around one goal

technical.question: How do you write a good user story?
key_points: Format: As a [user] I want [goal] so that [benefit]
Small enough to complete in one sprint
Focuses on user need not implementation

behavioral.question: Tell me about a product feature that failed. What did you learn?
key_points: Honest description of what went wrong
User research or assumptions that were wrong
What you did differently next time

behavioral.question: Describe a time you had to say no to a stakeholder request. How did you handle it?
key_points: Offered an alternative or later timeline

situational.question: Engineering says a feature will take 3 months. Business wants it in 3 weeks. What do you do?
key_points: Understand what business truly needs vs wants
Explore MVP that delivers core value faster
Facilitate honest conversation between both sides
Set realistic expectations and document trade-offs

technical.question: What is the difference between SEO and SEM?
key_points: SEO: organic search ranking through content and backlinks
SEO is long-term SEM is immediate
Both aim to increase search visibility

technical.question: What is a conversion funnel and what are the typical stages?
key_points: Top of funnel: reach and brand awareness

technical.question: How would you measure the success of a marketing campaign?
key_points: Define KPIs before the campaign: CTR conversion rate CAC ROAS
Compare to baseline or control group
Measure short-term conversions and long-term brand lift

behavioral.question: Tell me about a campaign you ran that exceeded expectations. What drove the success?

situational.question: Your ad campaign is spending budget but not converting. What do you investigate first?
key_points: Check landing page relevance and load speed

technical.question: What is a BRD and what does it contain?
What is the difference between functional and non-functional requirements?
key_points: Functional: what the system should do (features behaviour)
Non-functional: how the system should perform (speed security scalability)
Both are required for complete requirements
Example: login feature vs login must respond in under 2 seconds

technical.question: What is a use case diagram and when would you use it?
key_points: UML diagram showing actors and system interactions
Visualises who does what with the system
Helps identify scope and missing requirements

behavioral.question: Tell me about a time requirements changed midway through a project. How did you manage it?
key_points: Assessed impact on scope timeline and cost

situational.question: Two stakeholders have conflicting requirements for the same feature. How do you resolve it?
key_points: Focus on underlying business need not positions
Escalate if needed with a documented recommendation

technical.question: What is the difference between revenue, profit, and cash flow?
key_points: Cash flow: actual cash moving in and out
A company can be profitable but cash flow negative

technical.question: Explain DCF (Discounted Cash Flow) valuation in simple terms.
key_points: Estimates present value of future cash flows
Sum of discounted future cash flows minus debt
Sensitive to assumptions about growth and discount rate

technical.question: What is EBITDA and why is it used?
key_points: Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation Amortisation
Used to compare companies regardless of financing or accounting
Does not account for capital expenditure

behavioral.question: Tell me about a financial model you built. What was the most complex part?
key_points: Purpose and scope of the model
How it was used to drive a decision

situational.question: You find a significant error in a financial report that has already been sent to leadership. What do you do?
key_points: Prepare a corrected version with explanation
Be transparent about impact and cause

technical.question: What is the difference between UX and UI design?
key_points: UX: the overall experience and journey of the user
UI: the visual look and interactive elements
UX is research-driven UI is execution
Good UI can still have bad UX and vice versa

technical.question: What is a usability test and how do you conduct one?
key_points: 5 users can reveal ~85% of usability issues
Note failures confusion and time taken

technical.question: Explain the double diamond design process.
key_points: Discover: research and understand the problem space
Define: narrow down to the core problem
Develop: generate and prototype many ideas
Deliver: test and refine the best solution

behavioral.question: Tell me about a design decision you made based on user research that surprised you.

situational.question: A developer says your design is too complex to build in time. How do you respond?
key_points: Identify which parts of the design are non-negotiable for UX
Propose a simplified version that preserves core usability
Agree to revisit full design in next iteration

technical.question: What is the difference between a lead, a prospect, and an opportunity?
key_points: Lead: someone who has shown interest but not qualified
Prospect: qualified lead who fits ideal customer profile
Stages in a CRM sales pipeline

technical.question: What is SPIN selling and when would you apply it?
key_points: Helps customer articulate their own need

behavioral.question: Tell me about your biggest sales win. What did you do to close it?
key_points: How you built trust with the buyer

behavioral.question: Describe a time you lost a deal. What did you learn?
key_points: Honest reflection on what went wrong

situational.question: A prospect goes silent after a promising demo. How do you re-engage them?
key_points: Send a value-add follow-up not just a check-in
Reference specific pain point from the demo
Know when to move on vs persist

technical.question: What is keyword intent and why does it matter for content strategy?
key_points: Matching content type to intent improves ranking
Informational intent: blog posts and guides

technical.question: What is the difference between on-page and off-page SEO?
key_points: On-page: title tags headers content keyword usage
Off-page: backlinks domain authority social signals
Off-page depends on external sites linking to you

behavioral.question: Tell me about a piece of content you created that performed really well. Why do you think it worked?
key_points: Key factor: angle research format or timing

situational.question: You have to write about a technical topic you know nothing about. How do you approach it?
key_points: Interview a subject matter expert if possible
Get a technical review before publishing

technical.question: What is the difference between native, hybrid, and cross-platform mobile development?
key_points: Native: Swift/Kotlin for best performance but two codebases
Cross-platform: one codebase compiles to native (Flutter React Native)

technical.question: What is the activity lifecycle in Android or view controller lifecycle in iOS?
key_points: Android: onCreate onStart onResume onPause onStop onDestroy
Understand when to load data and release resources
Memory leaks often caused by ignoring lifecycle

technical.question: How do you handle background tasks on mobile without draining the battery?
key_points: Use platform-native APIs: WorkManager Android, BGTaskScheduler iOS
Avoid wake locks and excessive polling

behavioral.question: Tell me about a mobile app feature you built. What was the trickiest part?

situational.question: Your app crashes only on a specific device model you don't own. How do you debug it?
key_points: Use crash reporting tools: Firebase Crashlytics Sentry
Reproduce with emulator matching OS and screen size
Check device-specific APIs or screen densities
Ask a user for logs or beta access to their device
